summary: Iteration 2 - Full regression for guest-only checkout + admin auth flow. Backend: 18/18 pytest PASS (guest checkout creates Stripe session, validates email/cart/product, public status endpoint, removed /orders/mine returns 404, admin auth gates pending-count/orders/customers, admin endpoints return guest customer fields). Frontend: Home loads with NO login/register links in header; cart → /checkout directly (no auth gate); checkout form has all 5 data-testids and validates invalid email with toast 'Ingresa un email válido'; admin login works; AdminDashboard shows pending-orders-bell; AdminOrders shows customer name/email and row-expand reveals order-email/phone/address testids; removed routes (/login, /registro, /mis-pedidos) render empty (no route match).

minor.issue: Falls back to {status:pending} when Stripe test session not retrievable (emergent test key). Endpoint correctly returns 200 with fallback payload - acceptable but note 'Stripe limitation' from iteration 1 is now gracefully handled.

issues: 'Pedidos pagados' and 'Clientes' tiles show 0 even though guest orders exist (because none are payment_status=paid yet). Acceptable; sales/customers metrics correctly only count paid. Consider also showing total orders/initiated count for visibility.
Third-party tawk.to chat widget blocked by CORS (console errors). Not a regression - external service config issue.

action_items: (Optional/low) Dashboard could surface count of 'pending' (initiated) orders alongside paid count so admin sees real activity.
(Optional) CORS allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True is invalid per spec - already flagged in iter1, still present in server.py.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: on_event startup/shutdown still uses deprecated FastAPI events - migrate to lifespan.
server.py CORS: allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True is rejected by browsers per spec; should use explicit CORS_ORIGINS env from .env (CORS_ORIGINS='*' is read but not used).
server.py: /orders/status falls back to pending on Stripe exception - good resilience but logs swallow the error. Add logger.warning for debugging.
bcrypt used ($2b$ hashes confirmed), httpOnly secure cookies set with samesite='none' - correct for cross-site admin login.
seed_admin updates password if env password changes - idempotent and matches playbook.
Tests fixture for 'http' session reuses cookies across tests; introduced 'fresh_session' fixture for unauthenticated assertions - documented for future agents.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of testable flows (Stripe redirect not automatable)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@mundoinfantil.com / admin123 - WORKING. No customer auth (removed by design).
seed_data_creation: Several TEST_ prefixed orders created in MongoDB during checkout tests (test_guest@example.com, adminview@test.com, custagg@test.com, status@example.com, noauth@example.com, pending@test.com). Pending-count test cleans up its own paid order; other test orders remain as payment_status='initiated' and don't affect bell counts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_guest_checkout.py (pytest). All 18 pass. Use 'fresh_session' fixture for unauthenticated assertions; 'http' session shares admin cookie after first auth. To test bell badge>0 visually, insert order via mongo with payment_status='paid' status='pending' (see test_admin_pending_count_returns_count). Stripe test key works for session creation but cannot complete redirects in automation. Frontend testids confirmed: checkout-{name,email,phone,address}, pay-now-btn, checkout-btn, pending-orders-bell, pending-orders-badge, pending-orders-alert, order-{email,phone,address}-{id}, admin-login-{email,password,submit}.
rca of the issue: N/A - no regressions. Previous iteration 1 reported /api/orders/checkout broken due to removed user variable; verified fixed in this run. Customer auth (/login, /registro, /mis-pedidos) successfully removed - routes no longer render. Stripe status endpoint now has try/except fallback so it no longer 500s.
